phases.title: Phase 0 — Walking skeleton: OrganizationController migrated end-to-end
phases.scope: Mikado step 1+2 of DESIGN DWD-2, scoped to OrganizationController only. (1) Add module-private `_default_organization_uc()` factory to backend/app/controllers/organization_controller.py whose body performs the deferred `from app.use_cases import organization` import. (2) Add `*, _use_cases=_default_organization_uc` keyword-only parameter to both public methods on OrganizationController (post_organization, get_my_organization). (3) Replace the existing `_uc()` getter call expressions in those methods with `_use_cases().<method>(...)`. (4) Rewrite the 7 patches in backend/tests/controllers/test_organization_controller_char.py from `@patch('app.controllers.http_controller.organization_use_cases')` to passing `_use_cases=lambda: mock_uc` as a keyword argument; assertion lines remain byte-identical (DESIGN DWD-6 Iron Rule). (5) The 18-line alias block in http_controller.py is NOT yet deleted — other controllers' tests still patch it. The `from app.use_cases import organization as organization_use_cases` line stays until Phase 01 completes the rollout. (6) Wire the walking-skeleton acceptance scenario green by replacing the pytest.fail bodies in steps/controller_di_steps.py for the WS scenario only (the four step bodies under the 'Walking skeleton — get_my_organization with fake injected' header).
scenarios_to_unskip: tests/acceptance/refactor-controller-use-case-injection/walking-skeleton.feature :: Engineer injects a fake use-case factory into get_my_organization and the controller returns the fake's data
phases.files_changed_estimate: backend/app/controllers/organization_controller.py — replace `_uc()` getter with `_default_organization_uc` factory; add `*, _use_cases=_default_organization_uc` to 2 methods (-5 LOC, +10 LOC; ~10 LOC delta). backend/tests/controllers/test_organization_controller_char.py — rewrite 7 patches as kwarg injections (mechanical L1 transform; ~25 LOC delta in fixture mechanism, zero assertion edits per DESIGN DWD-6). tests/acceptance/refactor-controller-use-case-injection/steps/controller_di_steps.py — replace pytest.fail bodies for the WS scenario's 4 step bindings (~25 LOC of step bodies). Total: ~60 LOC across 3 files. Estimated size: S.
exit_criteria: Walking-skeleton scenario GREEN: OrganizationController.get_my_organization invoked with `_use_cases=lambda: fake` returns the fake's data in the JSON:API envelope, and the fake's get_organization mock recorded the engineer's user identity

Pre-existing test_organization_controller_char.py 7 tests still GREEN through the new `_use_cases=lambda: mock_uc` mechanism (Iron Rule audit: assertion lines byte-identical to origin/main; only fixture-mechanism lines changed)
All other backend tests still GREEN — the alias block on http_controller.py is intact, so untouched controllers still work via `_uc()` getter + module patch
OrganizationController's two methods both have `_use_cases` as keyword-only, defaulting to `_default_organization_uc`
`grep 'def _uc()' backend/app/controllers/organization_controller.py` returns nothing (the getter for THIS controller is gone)
`grep 'def _default_organization_uc' backend/app/controllers/organization_controller.py` returns one match

phases.title: Phase 1 — Milestone 1: kwarg injection rolled out to remaining 7 controllers
phases.scope: Mikado steps 1+2 of DESIGN DWD-2 applied to the remaining 7 per-aggregate controllers. (1) Add `_default_<aggregate>_uc` factory + `*, _use_cases=_default_<aggregate>_uc` keyword-only parameter to every public method on the 5 simple controllers: report_controller (5 methods), project_controller (5 methods), query_engine_controller (3 methods), sql_access_controller (5 methods), view_controller (5 methods). (2) For dataset_controller (multi-factory case per upstream-changes.md §3): add three factories `_default_dataset_uc`, `_default_upload_uc`, `_default_search_uc` and route each of the 10 public methods to its matching factory via `_use_cases=_default_<role>_uc`. (3) For conversation_controller (submodule-alias case): add five factories `_default_get_project_memory_uc`, `_default_create_session_uc`, `_default_list_sessions_uc`, `_default_list_session_events_uc`, `_default_update_session_uc` and route each of the 5 public methods to its matching factory. (4) Replace the existing `_uc()` getter call expressions in every method body with `_use_cases().<method>(...)`. (5) Wire milestone-1-kwarg-injection.feature scenarios green by replacing the pytest.fail bodies in steps/controller_di_steps.py under the 'Milestone 1' header (Scenario Outline + multi-factory + submodule-aliased + injected-failure scenarios). (6) The alias block in http_controller.py is NOT yet deleted — Phase 02 finishes the test rewrite and Phase 02's terminal commit deletes the alias block + the 8 `_uc()` getters atomically. NOTE: per DISTILL DWD-6, this Phase 01 introduces the factories + kwargs but does NOT yet delete the alias block; the per-controller test rewrites are interleaved here (ahead of the bulk Phase 02 sweep) only when needed to keep `./tools/test/test.sh --backend` green between commits.

phases.title: Phase 2 — Milestone 2: bundled test rewrite + atomic alias-block deletion
phases.scope: DESIGN DWD-2's exit criterion + Mikado step 5. (1) Bulk-rewrite the remaining patches in backend/tests/controllers/ (test_http_controller.py: 25 patches; test_dataset_controller_char.py: 16 patches; test_project_controller_char.py: 9 patches; test_analytics_controller_char.py: 25 patches; test_sql_access_controller_char.py: 14 patches; test_query_engine_controller_char.py: 9 patches; test_conversation_controller_char.py: N patches). Each rewrite: remove `@patch('app.controllers.http_controller.<x>_use_cases')` decorator, remove `mock_uc` parameter from method signature, add local `mock_uc = MagicMock()` inside the test body, pass `_use_cases=lambda: mock_uc` (or the matching factory-role kwarg for multi-factory dataset tests) on the controller call. Iron Rule per DESIGN DWD-6: assertion lines (`assert ...`, `assert_awaited_once_with(...)`, `assert_called_with(...)`) are byte-identical before and after. (2) Once every test is migrated, atomically: (a) delete the 18-line alias block in backend/app/controllers/http_controller.py (lines 26-41 today: every `from app.use_cases import * as *_use_cases` and every submodule-alias `from app.use_cases.* import * as *_uc`); (b) update the http_controller.py docstring to remove the 'Do NOT remove any of the module-level aliases' warning. (3) The HTTPController class itself, the `_serialize` / `_error_response` re-exports, and all 41 router call-sites are NOT touched — DESIGN DWD-4 + DWD-5 binding. (4) Wire milestone-2-test-migration.feature scenarios green by replacing pytest.fail bodies in steps/controller_di_steps.py under the 'Milestone 2' header.

phases.title: Phase 3 — Milestone 3: pytest-archon architectural enforcement (Rules A/B/C)
phases.scope: DESIGN DWD-2 step 4 + DESIGN DWD-8 (the three-rule architectural fence). (1) Create backend/tests/architecture/test_controller_imports.py with three pytest-archon rules. (2) Rule A (anti-regression): `app.controllers.http_controller` MUST NOT import any name from `app.use_cases` at module level. Detects re-introduction of the alias block. (3) Rule B (positive structural assertion): every per-aggregate controller module under backend/app/controllers/ MUST define at least one `_default_*_uc` callable AND every public method on its controller class MUST have a keyword-only `_use_cases` parameter whose default is one of those callables. AST walk over `<aggregate>_controller.py` files. Detects accidental removal of the injection point. (4) Rule C (γ-prevention): no source file under backend/app/routers/ MAY contain `Depends(<X>Controller` for any controller class. Negative grep over routers. Captures the FastAPI Depends non-interaction guarantee per DESIGN DWD-3. (5) Wire milestone-3-architectural-enforcement.feature scenarios green by replacing pytest.fail bodies in steps/controller_di_steps.py under the 'Milestone 3' header. The synthetic-violator scenarios construct candidate files in tmp_path and feed them through the same pytest-archon machinery; the clean-tree scenarios run the rule against the legitimate post-refactor source.

deliver_dispatch_notes.paradigm: Python — module-private factory functions + keyword-only-with-default parameter pattern (Option α per ADR-023). No new classes; controllers remain stateless `@staticmethod` collections per their existing shape. The `_default_<aggregate>_uc` factory bodies are 2-line lazy-import stubs that mirror the existing `_uc()` getter bodies byte-for-byte (only the function name and the consumer-side wiring change). Per DISTILL DWD-3, the only adapter exercised is the Python kwarg-default-binding mechanism — there is no substrate, no DB, no compose stack involved.
deliver_dispatch_notes.iron_rule: NEVER modify a characterization-test assertion line to make the kwarg-injection refactor green. Per DESIGN DWD-6 + DISTILL DWD-4, the migration is a known L1 test-refactoring transform: substitute fixture mechanism without altering assertions. Allowed diffs in any test file: removed `@patch(...)` decorators; removed `mock_uc` parameter names from method signatures; added local `mock_uc = MagicMock()` lines; added `_use_cases=lambda: mock_uc` kwarg arguments. Assertion lines (`assert ...`, `assert_awaited_once_with(...)`, `assert_called_with(...)`) MUST be byte-identical to origin/main. After 3 failed attempts to keep a single test green, revert and escalate; do NOT modify the assertion.
deliver_dispatch_notes.tdd_cycle: RED_ACCEPTANCE → unskip one scenario → RED_UNIT (the per-aggregate controller's existing characterization tests serve as the unit-level safety net during Phase 00/01) → GREEN → minimum implementation (factory + kwarg per the controller method that the unskipped scenario exercises) → REFACTOR per RPP L1-L6 → next scenario. Phase 02's bulk test rewrite is mechanical L1 (no new RED unit tests; the existing test bodies define the GREEN target). Phase 03 introduces fresh RED unit-level pytest-archon rules.
